steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage cites 'the sixth most commonly used password' as evidence. The blank describes the type of password users pick for convenience. The word must match what that ranking statistic proves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — creative: directly contradicts the point — users choosing convenience avoid complexity. ✗
  • Choice B — useful: the evidence is about frequency/popularity, not usefulness. ✗
  • Choice C — complex: this contradicts the passage's point that users choose easy-to-remember, convenience-oriented passwords. ✗
  • Choice D — popular: 'sixth most commonly used' is a ranking of popularity. The word matches the evidence exactly. ✓
That is the correct answer.
